title: ajuga chamaecistus subsp. tomentella
intro: Ajuga chamaecistus subsp. tomentella, also known as Ajuga or Bugleweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and northwest Africa. The medicinal properties of Ajuga include its ability to inhibit the conversion of thyroxine to triiodothyronine, which can help to regulate an overactive thyroid gland, as well as its antiviral and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ajuga include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety and stress, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In herbal preparations, Ajuga is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is typically used in small doses due to its potential interactions with thyroid medications.
